title: Final manual release review for Hermes public blueprint
summary: The blueprint is now coherent enough for a conservative public release. Remaining gaps are optional refinements rather than blockers, provided the current bounded claim posture is preserved.

why: Narrative and evidence posture are now strong enough for scrutiny and coherent enough for a bounded public release.
The DE/EN consistency pass, polish pass, and confirmatory go/no-go check are complete.
Public/private boundary language remains adequate as long as the current bounded claim posture is preserved.

review_checks.note: Current wording remains conservative after the DE/EN pass, polish pass, and confirmatory review.
Private/public evidence boundary is disclosed clearly enough for the current release posture.
Canonical reading path and reader routing now carry the story well.
Landing page and README are now polished enough for a bounded public release.
Prominence remains controlled: visible but bounded.

non_blocking_items: Reduce raw file-path density further if a more productized landing page is desired.
Expand the evidence package later if stronger public proof is desired.
Run future stylistic iteration only if the repo scope broadens.
